rule: A receipt is only official if it exists in the gateway PostgreSQL ledger and passes hash verification.
reason: Prevents ledger conflicts and ensures a single auditable source of truth.

phase_a.description: Internal engine operates independently. Gateway client module built and tested. Governance router dispatch layer wired into tRPC.

phase_b.description: Gateway URL set. Write operations route through gateway (fail-closed). Read operations fall back to internal engine. Both ledgers active during transition.
phase_b.blocker: Permanent gateway URL not yet confirmed (Azure deployment DNS not resolving; Railway deployment in progress)

phase_c.description: Internal ledger deprecated. All reads and writes go through gateway PostgreSQL. Demo site ledger entries migrated to gateway. Single source of truth fully enforced.

migration_required.note: All 5 demo site ledger entries (including genesis) must be migrated to gateway PostgreSQL before Phase C activation
